task_completion.description: Verify that the Aircraft model in models.py was updated with the new field.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing /opt/aerobridge/registry/models.py with 'max_altitude_m = models.IntegerField(null=True, blank=True, help_text='Maximum permitted flight altitude in meters')' added to the Aircraft class.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Aircraft admin configuration in admin.py was updated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing /opt/aerobridge/registry/admin.py with 'max_altitude_m' added to the Aircraft admin class (e.g., in list_display, fields, or fieldsets).

task_completion.description: Verify that database migrations were created and applied.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the execution of 'python manage.py makemigrations' and 'python manage.py migrate' with successful output indicating the max_altitude_m field was added.

task_completion.description: Verify that existing Aircraft records were populated with the default value.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a Python script, Django shell session, or SQL commands updating existing Aircraft records to set max_altitude_m to 120.

task_completion.description: Verify that the new field is visible in the Django admin panel and shows the correct value.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Web browser open to the Django admin panel (http://localhost:8000/admin/registry/aircraft/ or an individual aircraft's edit page) showing the 'max_altitude_m' column or field, with the value 120 visible.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually modified the Python files rather than just printing the expected code.
integrity.visual_evidence: File editor saving the changes or terminal commands like 'cat' showing the modified file contents.

integrity.description: Ensure the admin panel verification is from a real browser rendering the Django admin interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: A standard web browser UI (like Firefox or Chromium) displaying the styled Django admin page, not just HTML source code or a text dump.

integrity.description: Ensure the Django development server was running to reflect the changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the 'runserver' command active, or the browser successfully loading the localhost:8000 URL without connection errors.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires adding 'max_altitude_m' to the Aircraft model. The default value for existing records must be exactly 120. The field must be visible in the Django admin panel at http://localhost:8000/admin/registry/aircraft/.
